goal: Wire three coordinated MCP tools (`get_architecture`, `trace_dependencies`, `blast_radius`) â€” all G4-primary per master-plan Â§3.2 rows 8/9/10 â€” through the F09 `execute_g4` orchestrator (WO-0073, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g4.rs`) so each tool returns a structured architecture-side envelope backed by the same dependency-inversion seam (`G4Source` trait, DEC-0008 Â§4) that F09 already exposes. Adds three sibling handlers (`handle_get_architecture`, `handle_trace_dependencies`, `handle_blast_radius`) + a `g4_sources: Option<Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>>>` field on `McpServer` + `with_g4_sources()` builder + three frozen `#[tokio::test]` selectors at module root in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (`server::test_get_architecture_tool`, `server::test_trace_dependencies_tool`, `server::test_blast_radius_tool`). Each test injects deterministic in-process `TestG4Source` impls (DEC-0008 Â§4 dependency-inversion; pattern from `executor::test_g4_architecture_query` at `executor.rs:3790`) so the WO ships fully self-contained with NO real CodeGraphContext / LSP-call-hierarchy production wiring (deferred to a follow-up WO per the WO-0072/0077 production-wiring scope_out conventions).
plan_summary: Three-feature bundle around the shared G4 orchestration backbone, mirroring the WO-0067 (classifier+reason parser around Â§6.2/Â§8.6) / WO-0068 (executor+fusion around Â§6.1/Â§6.2) / WO-0070 (G3 parallel merge with TestG3Source) coordinated-features template. (1) Extend `McpServer` with `pub g4_sources: Option<Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>>>` + builder method `with_g4_sources(sources: Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>>) -> Self` (chainable off `new`/`with_knowledge_graph`/`with_g2_sources`/`with_find_similar_executor`). (2) Add three async handlers â€” `handle_get_architecture(id, params, sources)`, `handle_trace_dependencies(id, params, sources)`, `handle_blast_radius(id, params, sources)` â€” each builds a `G4Query` from MCP `arguments`, invokes `execute_g4`, projects `G4Outcome` into a tool-specific JSON envelope. (3) Wire dispatch in `handle_tools_call` for tool names `get_architecture` / `trace_dependencies` / `blast_radius` (only when `g4_sources` is `Some`; otherwise fall through to the phase-1 `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub per invariant #9). (4) Three frozen `#[tokio::test]` tests at module root with deterministic `TestG4Source` impls (mirroring `executor.rs:3822` `TestG4Source` / `executor.rs:3790` `test_g4_architecture_query` shape). (5) Pre-baked M1/M2/M3 mutation contract â€” one mutation per feature (see scope_in #18-#20). Single feat commit lands the three handlers + the field + the builder + the three tests as one cohesive unit per DEC-0005 module-coherence (the M1/M2/M3 mutation-restoration contract requires all three sites to coexist in the same diff). Estimated total LOC: ~700-900 (three handlers ~80 LOC each, three tests ~120-180 LOC each including TestG4Source helpers, +builder/field/dispatch ~30 LOC).
scope_in: 1. Extend `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` `McpServer` struct: add a new optional field `pub g4_sources: Option<Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>>>`. Mirror the `g2_sources` / `find_similar` field shape verbatim â€” `Option<Arc<...>>` so callers can attach the source list after construction without forcing an existing call-site refactor. Update the four existing constructor paths (`new`, `with_knowledge_graph`, `with_g2_sources`, `with_find_similar_executor`) to default `g4_sources` to `None`. Phase-1 invariant #9 (the 19 unwired tools fall through to `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true`) is preserved for `get_architecture` / `trace_dependencies` / `blast_radius` whenever `g4_sources.is_none()`.

2. Add a chainable builder method `pub fn with_g4_sources(mut self, sources: Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>>) -> Self` that sets `self.g4_sources = Some(sources)` and returns `self` (mirror `with_g2_sources` / `with_find_similar_executor` precedent). Rustdoc cites master-plan Â§3.2 rows 8/9/10 + DEC-0008 Â§4 (UCIL-internal trait boundary so the daemon's startup orchestrator can attach a `Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>` consisting of `CodeGraphContextG4Source` + `LSPCallHierarchyG4Source` (production wiring deferred per WO-0072 scope_out + WO-0073 production-wiring deferral).

3. Add three sibling async handlers in the existing `impl McpServer { ... }` block (under `handle_search_code`/`handle_find_similar`): `async fn handle_get_architecture(id: &Value, params: &Value, sources: &Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>>) -> Value` (P3-W10-F16 / master-plan Â§3.2 row 8 / Â§18 Phase 3 Week 10 line 1812). Read MCP `arguments`: optional `target` (string seed module/file/symbol; if absent, treat as project-root scan), optional `max_depth` (u32, default 3, clamped to [0, 8]), optional `max_edges` (usize, default 256, clamped to [0, 4096]). Construct `G4Query { changed_nodes: vec![target.unwrap_or_default()], max_blast_depth: max_depth, max_edges }`. Call `crate::g4::execute_g4(sources, query).await`. Project the resulting `G4Outcome` into the response envelope: `_meta.tool == "get_architecture"`, `_meta.source == "g4-architecture-fanout"`, `_meta.modules` (sorted unique `source` and `target` symbols from `unioned.unified_edges` â€” the architecture surface), `_meta.edges` (the `unified_edges[]` projected as `{source, target, edge_kind, contributing_source_ids, coupling_weight}`), `_meta.master_timed_out`, `_meta.source_results` (per-source `{source_id, status, edge_count, elapsed_ms}` tuples), `_meta.token_count` (advisory only). `content[0].text` is a one-line summary `"architecture overview: N modules, M edges from K sources"`.

4. `handle_trace_dependencies` (P3-W10-F17 / master-plan Â§3.2 row 9). Read MCP `arguments`: required `target` (string symbol/file), optional `direction` (string, one of `"upstream"` / `"downstream"` / `"both"`, default `"both"`), optional `max_depth` (u32, default 3, clamped to [0, 8]). Construct `G4Query { changed_nodes: vec![target], max_blast_depth: max_depth, max_edges: 1024 }`. Invoke `execute_g4`. The outcome's `unioned.blast_radius` carries depth-annotated downstream nodes (BFS following edges where target is `source`); compute the upstream chain by symmetric BFS over edges where target is `target` (i.e., reverse the edge direction in a pure-functional pass over `unioned.unified_edges`). Project the response envelope: `_meta.tool == "trace_dependencies"`, `_meta.source == "g4-architecture-fanout"`, `_meta.target` (echo), `_meta.direction` (echo), `_meta.upstream` (only when direction in {"upstream", "both"} â€” array of `{node, depth}` BFS-derived from reverse edges, sorted by depth ascending then node ascending), `_meta.downstream` (only when direction in {"downstream", "both"} â€” array of `{node, depth}` from `blast_radius` BFS, sorted same), `_meta.master_timed_out`, `_meta.source_results`, `_meta.token_count`. `content[0].text` is a one-line summary `"trace_dependencies: U upstream + D downstream nodes (target='X', max_depth=N)"`. Missing/non-string `target` â†’ JSON-RPC error -32602 (invalid params) per the `find_definition` precedent.

5. `handle_blast_radius` (P3-W10-F18 / master-plan Â§3.2 row 10). Read MCP `arguments`: required `target` (string OR array-of-strings â€” accept both shapes; convert single string to `vec![s]`), optional `max_depth` (u32, default 3, clamped to [0, 8]), optional `max_edges` (usize, default 1024, clamped to [0, 4096]). Construct `G4Query { changed_nodes, max_blast_depth: max_depth, max_edges }`. Invoke `execute_g4`. The outcome's `unioned.blast_radius` is the BFS-derived impacted-nodes list with `coupling_weight`-multiplicative path weighting per master-plan Â§5.4 line 495 â€” F09 already implements this in `merge_g4_dependency_union`. Project response envelope: `_meta.tool == "blast_radius"`, `_meta.source == "g4-architecture-fanout"`, `_meta.target` (echo of changed_nodes), `_meta.max_depth` (echo), `_meta.impacted` (array of `{node, depth, path_weight}` from `blast_radius`, sorted descending by `path_weight` then ascending by depth then ascending by node â€” coupling-weighted ranking per the master-plan), `_meta.dependency_chain` (top-K=10 paths from each seed to a leaf in the BFS tree, formatted as `["seed -> hop1 -> hop2 -> leaf", ...]`; empty when blast_radius is empty), `_meta.master_timed_out`, `_meta.source_results`, `_meta.token_count`. `content[0].text` is `"blast_radius: K impacted nodes from N seed(s), top weight=W (depth=D)"`.

6. Wire dispatch in `handle_tools_call` (currently around `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs:710-734`): immediately AFTER the `find_similar` block and BEFORE the phase-1 stub fall-through, insert three guarded blocks of the same shape: `if name == "get_architecture" { if let Some(srcs) = self.g4_sources.as_ref() { return Self::handle_get_architecture(id, params, srcs).await; } }` (and likewise for `trace_dependencies` / `blast_radius`). When `g4_sources.is_none()`, control falls through to the `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub path so phase-1 invariant #9 is preserved AND the existing `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` selector remains wire-compatible (the catalog count is unchanged at 22).

7. Add THREE frozen `#[tokio::test]` tests at module root inside `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`'s existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` block â€” BUT placed at MODULE ROOT (NOT nested under any inner `mod`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0068 lessons Â§planner: 'frozen-test selector substring-match REQUIRES module-root placement'. Selector substring-match `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_get_architecture_tool` (and the two siblings) MUST resolve uniquely without `--exact`. Confirm via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_get_architecture_tool 2>&1 | grep -E 'running|test result'` showing `1 passed; 0 failed`.

8. Each test constructs an in-process `TestG4Source` impl (mirror `executor::test_g4_architecture_query`'s `TestG4Source` at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs:3822`) declaring a deterministic dependency graph. Build a `Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>` of one or more TestG4Source impls, wrap in `Arc::new(...)`, attach via `McpServer::new().with_g4_sources(...)`. Drive `handle_tools_call` (or the underlying handler directly) with a constructed `params` payload, decode the response, assert on `_meta.*` fields. Use `serde_json::json!(...)` for both inputs and assertion-side expected envelopes.

9. `test_get_architecture_tool` (P3-W10-F16 frozen test). Inject a `TestG4Source` whose `execute()` returns four edges: `A -> B (Import, 0.9)`, `B -> C (Call, 0.7)`, `C -> D (Implements, 0.6)`, `A -> D (Inherits, 0.4)`. Construct `params = {"name": "get_architecture", "arguments": {"target": "A", "max_depth": 4, "max_edges": 256}}`. Sub-assertions (each panic message uses the SA-numbered `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` format per DEC-0007 + WO-0067 lessons): SA1 `_meta.tool == "get_architecture"`, SA2 `_meta.source == "g4-architecture-fanout"`, SA3 `_meta.modules` is a sorted unique list containing `["A", "B", "C", "D"]`, SA4 `_meta.edges.len() == 4`, SA5 the highest-weight edge's `coupling_weight == 0.9` (the `A -> B` import edge), SA6 `_meta.master_timed_out == false`, SA7 `_meta.source_results.len() == 1` (one TestG4Source), SA8 `_meta.source_results[0].status == "available"`.

10. `test_trace_dependencies_tool` (P3-W10-F17 frozen test). Inject a `TestG4Source` whose graph yields a small chain plus a fork: `A -> B (Call, 0.9)`, `B -> C (Call, 0.8)`, `C -> D (Call, 0.7)`, `E -> B (Call, 0.6)`. Drive `params = {"name": "trace_dependencies", "arguments": {"target": "B", "direction": "both", "max_depth": 3}}`. Sub-assertions: SA1 `_meta.tool == "trace_dependencies"`, SA2 `_meta.target == "B"`, SA3 `_meta.direction == "both"`, SA4 `_meta.upstream.len() == 1` AND `_meta.upstream[0] == {"node": "A", "depth": 1}` (the only upstream â€” `A -> B`; `E -> B` is also upstream and SA4 is amended to `len() == 2` with both A@depth=1 AND E@depth=1, sorted alphabetically), SA5 `_meta.downstream` contains both `{"node": "C", "depth": 1}` AND `{"node": "D", "depth": 2}` sorted by depth ascending, SA6 `_meta.master_timed_out == false`, SA7 a separate sub-test calls `params` with `direction = "upstream"` and asserts `_meta.downstream` is absent (or null) â€” direction-filtering is load-bearing.

11. `test_blast_radius_tool` (P3-W10-F18 frozen test). Inject a `TestG4Source` whose graph yields a 5-node BFS tree: `A -> B (Import, 0.9)`, `A -> C (Call, 0.5)`, `B -> D (Call, 0.8)`, `C -> E (Call, 0.4)`. Drive `params = {"name": "blast_radius", "arguments": {"target": "A", "max_depth": 3, "max_edges": 256}}`. Sub-assertions: SA1 `_meta.tool == "blast_radius"`, SA2 `_meta.target == ["A"]` (single-string target lifted to array), SA3 `_meta.impacted.len() == 4` (B, C, D, E â€” A itself is the seed and excluded from impacted per F09 BFS contract), SA4 `_meta.impacted[0].node` (highest path_weight) is one of `B` / `D` (the heavier-weighted import-chain â€” `A -> B = 0.9` and `A -> B -> D = 0.9 * 0.8 = 0.72` per multiplicative weighting), SA5 `_meta.impacted[0].path_weight >= _meta.impacted[1].path_weight` (descending sort invariant), SA6 a separate sub-test drives `params` with `target = ["A", "C"]` (array shape) and asserts `_meta.target == ["A", "C"]` (array preserved), SA7 `_meta.dependency_chain.len() >= 1` AND each entry is a non-empty string containing `" -> "` substring (path-shape canary), SA8 `_meta.master_timed_out == false`.

12. The `TestG4Source` impl MUST live INSIDE the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` block in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` â€” under `#[cfg(test)]` so it compiles only in test profile. Test source code is exempt from the production-side word-ban grep (per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 carve-out). Each test may define its own `TestG4Source` OR they may share a single `TestG4Source` helper whose `Vec<G4DependencyEdge>` is parameterised by a constructor â€” the latter is more compact AND more aligned with WO-0070's pattern. Either shape is acceptable; document the choice in the rustdoc adjacent to the helper.

13. The new field + builder + handlers + dispatch wiring + tests + TestG4Source helper SHOULD ship in ONE cohesive feat commit per DEC-0005 module-coherence (mirroring WO-0067 ceqp.rs at 548 LOC and WO-0068 cross_group.rs at 788 LOC). The M1/M2/M3 mutation-restoration contract requires all sites to coexist in the same diff â€” splitting helper-by-helper would produce stub-shaped intermediate states (a handler without a dispatch wire would not be reachable) AND a non-compilable test (with the field but without the builder method, or vice versa). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0067/0068 precedent in the commit body explicitly. Estimated LOC: ~700-900.

Annotate each with `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.tool.<tool_name>", skip_all, fields(target = ?args.target, max_depth = args.max_depth))]` (or equivalent) where `<tool_name>` is `get_architecture` / `trace_dependencies` / `blast_radius`. Cite Â§15.2 + WO-0070 lesson `For executor: Â§15.2 tracing on the orchestrator applies whenever async/IO is involved` (for the negative inverse: the WO-0067 /WO-0068 pure-deterministic carve-out does NOT apply here).

The three handlers' rustdoc MUST cite (a) the master-plan tool-spec row (Â§3.2 row 8/9/10 verbatim), (b) the feature ID (P3-W10-F16/F17/F18), (c) the WO ID (WO-0083), (d) the underlying G4 orchestrator function (`crate::g4::execute_g4`), (e) the dependency-inversion rationale (DEC-0008 Â§4 â€” UCIL-internal `G4Source` trait so production-wiring is decoupled from MCP-tool dispatch).

Cargo-test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_get_architecture_tool` MUST FAIL with the SA3 panic `(SA3) modules contains all four nodes; left: [], right: ["A", "B", "C", "D"]` (or analogous SA4 `_meta.edges.len() == 4` panic). Cargo-test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_blast_radius_tool` MUST FAIL with the SA5 panic `(SA5) impacted[0].path_weight >= impacted[1].path_weight; left: 0.36 (E), right: 0.72 (D)` (or analogous descending-sort invariant violation). Same md5 snapshot + restore as M1.

M1 = SA3 module-list emptiness OR SA4 edge-count mismatch (data-projection-zeroing failure mode); M2 = SA5 downstream-list emptiness (BFS-direction failure mode); M3 = SA5 sort-invariant violation (sort-direction failure mode). Three substantively distinct surfaces â€” verifier accepts. Pre-restoration md5sum match confirmed for each; reality-check.sh's pre-existing-stash bug is the authoritative anti-laziness layer per WO-0072/0073 lessons Â§verifier â€” `git stash` is NOT used; only `Edit` for in-place mutation + `git checkout --` for restore.

The frozen tests' SA-numbered panic messages MUST follow the format `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: <observed>; right: <expected>` per DEC-0007 + the established WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070/0072 pattern. Each `assert!` / `assert_eq!` carries a panic body that names the SA, identifies the semantic check, and includes both observed and expected values. Verifier reads the panic line, identifies the SA, restores the file. No grep for which test asserted what.

Optional args missing-or-malformed â†’ use the spec'd default. Out-of-range values (e.g., `max_depth > 8`) MUST clamp silently to the spec'd cap (NO error envelope) â€” clamping is the canonical UX per master-plan Â§3.2 + WO-0035 (search_code) precedent for `max_results`.

Confirm via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_all_22_tools_registered` returning green from a `cargo clean` baseline (this test was added in WO-0010 and is the canonical regression detector for the catalog count).

Confirm via a fourth optional sub-test (sibling to the three frozen tests) constructing `McpServer::new()` (no `with_g4_sources` call) and asserting `handle_tools_call` for `get_architecture` returns `_meta.not_yet_implemented == true`. Inner `Arc<dyn G4Source>` mirrors the boxed-trait pattern that `crate::g4::execute_g4` already accepts (the F09 signature takes `&[Arc<dyn G4Source>]`).

The examples may be `no_run` doctests (do not require the daemon to be live) or pure prose-comments â€” either is acceptable.

35. The three handler functions (`handle_get_architecture` etc.) are EXPOSED at module scope as `Self::handle_*` static-dispatch (mirror `Self::handle_find_definition` precedent at `server.rs:712`) â€” NOT free functions. They take `(id: &Value, params: &Value, sources: &Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>>) -> Value` (or `-> impl Future<Output = Value> + Send` for the async variants). Dispatch in `handle_tools_call` invokes them via `Self::handle_<tool>(id, params, srcs).await`.

36. The dispatch wiring MUST run AFTER the existing `find_similar` block and BEFORE the phase-1 stub fall-through `json!({...not_yet_implemented...})`. Order matters: each new tool name takes priority over the stub fall-through. The `find_similar` block sits before `// Phase-1 invariant #9: every tool handler is a stub...` comment block at server.rs:736; new blocks insert directly above that comment.

37. The TestG4Source helper (parameterised constructor returning a `Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>`) keeps test code compact AND lets each test inject a tailored graph. Parameterise by `Vec<G4DependencyEdge>` + `String source_id`; default `G4SourceStatus::Available` + zero `elapsed_ms` for the deterministic positive path. Future negative-path testing (per-source timeouts) is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO (deferred to integration-tier coverage).

38. Estimated commit cadence: ~3-4 commits total. (1) `feat(daemon): wire G4 source list onto McpServer + with_g4_sources builder` (the field + builder + dispatch hooks; ~50 LOC). (2) `feat(daemon): add handle_get_architecture (P3-W10-F16) + frozen test` (~250 LOC including TestG4Source helper). (3) `feat(daemon): add handle_trace_dependencies (P3-W10-F17) + frozen test` (~250 LOC reusing the helper). (4) `feat(daemon): add handle_blast_radius (P3-W10-F18) + frozen test` (~250 LOC). Each commit is verifiably-buildable in isolation (the dispatch hooks are gated by `g4_sources.is_some()`, so an unused field compiles cleanly) BUT per DEC-0005 module-coherence the entire suite SHOULD ship as fewer larger cohesive commits â€” so an alternative shape is `feat(daemon): wire three architecture MCP tools (P3-W10-F16/F17/F18)` as a SINGLE commit at ~700-900 LOC. The single-commit shape is preferred for the M1/M2/M3 mutation-restoration contract since all three sites coexist in one diff. Executor MAY choose whichever cadence supports clean compilation at every step â€” both are acceptable per DEC-0005 + WO-0067/0068 carve-out cited in the relevant commit body.

39. The SA-numbered assertion canon: every `assert!` / `assert_eq!` in the three frozen tests carries a `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic body. SA1..SA8 enumerate the load-bearing semantic checks per test. The verifier should be able to read a single panic line and identify (a) which test, (b) which SA, (c) what failed semantically. NO ceremonial assertions â€” every SA maps to a load-bearing check (data-projection completeness, BFS-direction correctness, sort-invariant ordering, response-shape canonicality, master-deadline non-trip on the deterministic positive path).

40. The DEC-0008 Â§4 dependency-inversion seam is the AUTHORITATIVE rationale for shipping these MCP tools without a live CodeGraphContext / LSP-call-hierarchy production-source impl. The MCP-tool surface lands NOW; the production-side `CodeGraphContextG4Source` / `LSPCallHierarchyG4Source` impls land in a follow-up WO that bundles the daemon-startup-orchestration wiring (paired with the WO-0072 plugin-runtime activation + WO-0073 G4 source registration). This is the SAME shape as G1Source (WO-0047), G2 sources (WO-0044/WO-0050/WO-0051), G3Source (WO-0070). This WO ships the MCP-tool dispatch surface fully self-contained with a deterministic test TestG4Source â€” production wiring is OUT OF SCOPE.

41. Phase 3 progress impact: with WO-0083 landing the three architecture MCP tools, Phase 3 reaches 24/45 = 53.3% complete (up from 21/45 = 46.7%). Subsequent planner passes (WO-0084+) target the remaining 21 unfinished features (P3-W9-F11 incremental test suite, P3-W10-F01 Aider repo-map, P3-W10-F04/F09/F10/F11/F12/F13/F14/F15 G5 Context query + conflict-resolution + bonus-context selector + multi-tier merging + warm processors + dependency-cruiser + Zoekt manifests, P3-W11-F01/F05/F06 G7 quality pipeline entry + severity-merge + quality_issues table, P3-W11-F09/F10/F11/F12/F13/F14/F15/F16 G8 test discovery + check_quality + review_changes + feedback loop + integration test suites + type_check + full-pipeline integration suite).

16. Do NOT introduce `unsafe` blocks. No `std::env::set_var`. No `unsafe impl Send/Sync`. The new handlers are pure-functional projections; thread-safety comes from `Arc + Send + Sync` bounds on `G4Source`.

Live stdio integration testing is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO; it lands as part of the production-wiring follow-up.
